scenario: A FEEL expression must compute the median of a list of numbers -- for statistical analysis. prices = [10, 15, 12, 18, 14] → median = 14.
question: Which FEEL built-in fits "compute median"?

options.text: mean(list) computes the median because it finds the central tendency of the data set -- this confuses two distinct statistical measures. mean() calculates the arithmetic average by summing all values and dividing by the count. median() finds the middle value of a sorted list, which is the 50th percentile. For skewed distributions, mean and median can differ substantially. The FEEL specification defines both as separate built-in functions with different computational semantics.

median(list) -- FEEL's built-in median function returns the middle value of a sorted list. For odd-length lists it returns the single middle element; for even-length lists it returns the arithmetic average of the two middle elements. median([10, 15, 12, 18, 14]) returns 14 (sorted: 10, 12, 14, 15, 18 → middle = 14). Companion functions: mean(list) for average; stddev(list) for standard deviation; mode(list) for most frequent values.

FEEL does not include a median function -- teams must manually sort a list and extract the middle element using list indexing operations -- this description does not reflect the actual behaviour. FEEL provides median(list) as a built-in list function that computes the middle value of the input list after sorting. The built-in handles both odd-length lists (returns the single middle element) and even-length lists (returns the average of the two middle elements). Reinventing this manually would produce more complex and error-prone expressions.

The manual approach requires calculating the list length, determining the middle index, and handling even vs odd lengths with separate logic. Using median(list) directly expresses the intent clearly in a single function call and is less prone to off-by-one indexing errors.

a.text: Incorrect. FEEL provides statistical functions for lists: mean(list): arithmetic mean (average). median(list): middle value; for even-length lists, average of two middles. stddev(list): standard deviation (sample). mode(list): most frequent value(s) -- returns list of modes. All handle lists of numbers. They handle edge cases (empty list, single element) gracefully or return null / error per spec. Use cases: mean: average calculations. median: robust central tendency (less sensitive to outliers than mean). stddev: variability measurement. mode: most common value.
